page: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highmem-112 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture, this server features 112 vCPUs mapped to 56 physical cores with simultaneous multithreading enabled. It is equipped with 896 GB of dedicated system memory, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per core. The processor includes a cache hierarchy consisting of 32 KB L1 data cache, 0.5 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. This instance does not include local NVMe storage or integrated GPU hardware.

Benchmark evaluations indicate top-tier performance in multi-core processing and memory subsystem operations. The instance achieves top 10% placement in stress-ng multi-core CPU tests and Passmark CPU metrics, including physics and encryption workloads. Memory performance is exceptionally strong, with cached read-only small block bandwidth reaching 7,672.69 GB/sec and uncached large block read bandwidth at 274.66 GB/sec. Database operations are highly efficient, as demonstrated by Redis SET performance reaching 120.12 million operations per second. However, single-threaded tasks and large-scale LLM text generation represent performance trade-offs, with 70B model text generation falling into the bottom 25% of benchmarks.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c2d-highmem-112 offers high resource density for memory-bound workloads without the added expense of bundled local storage or GPU accelerators. The combination of high memory capacity and strong multi-core performance makes this instance suitable for large-scale in-memory databases, high-throughput web serving, software compilation, and medium-scale LLM prompt processing. It is also well-suited for memory-intensive enterprise applications and data-processing pipelines that do not require local physical disks.
description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highmem-112 is a memory-optimized server instance featuring 112 vCPUs and 896 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor on the x86_64 architecture. With 56 physical cores and a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per core, this instance delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth, as evidenced by outstanding stress-ng and Passmark benchmarks. It excels in memory-intensive operations, achieving 120.12 million operations per second in Redis SET tests. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, making it a highly resource-dense option for memory-bound workloads. It is optimized for in-memory databases, heavy multi-threaded computing, software compilation, and high-throughput web serving, though it exhibits lower performance in single-threaded tasks and large-scale LLM text generation.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highmem-112 is a memory-optimized instance with 112 vCPUs and 896 GB RAM. Powered by AMD EPYC 7B13, it delivers top-tier multi-core performance and memory bandwidth for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highmem-112 features 112 vCPUs, 896 GB RAM, and AMD EPYC 7B13 processors, optimized for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
tagline: A high-density, memory-optimized virtual machine delivering top-tier multi-core performance and exceptional memory bandwidth for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with 112 dedicated vCPUs and 56 physical cores.
Equipped with 896 GB of RAM, providing a high 8 GB per core memory-to-vCPU ratio.
Top-tier multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth in benchmark testing.
Delivers 120.12 million operations per second in Redis SET database benchmarks.
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators, optimizing cost for pure memory workloads.
Best suited for in-memory databases, software compilation, and high-throughput web serving.
